steps.title: Separate truth from pretense
steps.content: Richard admits that he set the secret mischiefs in motion and that he has cast Clarence into darkness. At the same time, he says he weeps over Clarence to Derby, Hastings, and Buckingham and tells them the queen and her allies are responsible. The passage depends on the contrast between Richard's private guilt and public performance.

steps.title: Reject partial readings
steps.content: Choice A wrongly suggests Richard publicly attributes Clarence's trouble partly to his own plotting and misidentifies Derby, Hastings, and Buckingham as the queen's allies. Choice B captures the weeping but omits Richard's responsibility for Clarence's situation. Choice C invents a claim that Richard has kept the trust of the queen and her allies.

steps.title: Capture the deception
steps.content: Choice D includes the full pattern in the passage: Richard pretends to be aggrieved, blames the queen and her allies for Clarence's situation, and privately acknowledges that he is the one who caused the hostility toward Clarence. The answer is D.
